difficulty.rationale: Requires a durable version table, ordered one-time application of migrations including a non-idempotent ALTER that must be guarded so the second boot neither re-runs nor crashes, plus correct reporting and data persistence across restarts.

gates.name: security_baseline
gates.requirement: no hidden test access or network egress

must: On boot the app applies the three migrations in order, recorded in schema_migrations.
GET /schema/version returns version 3 and the applied list in order.
POST /users persists a user with name and email.
Users and the migration version survive a process restart.
must_not: Re-apply a migration that is already recorded.
Crash on a later boot by re-running the ALTER.
Store schema state or user data only in memory.
edge_cases: A second and third boot keep version at 3 and do not re-run migrations.

reward_model.description: Harbor reward.txt is computed by the bun-bench gate model in tests/test.sh.
